data.title: Dow steadies as Fed holds rate; Tesla earnings awaited. Investor's Business Daily covers the updates.
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones index showed fluctuations on Wednesday following the Federal Reserve's decision to maintain its key rate. Investors are eagerly awaiting Tesla's earnings report later today. The market remains stable as the Dow shows no significant change. This event is covered live by Investor's Business Daily, providing real-time updates on the day's market performance and key happenings. Stay tuned for more information on how these developments will impact the financial landscape.

data.title: KBRA assigns ratings to $331.1M mortgage loans in Provident Funding Mortgage Trust 2026-1 transaction.
data.description: In New York, KBRA has assigned preliminary ratings to 38 classes of mortgage pass-through certificates from Provident Funding Mortgage Trust 2026-1 (PFMT 2026-1). PFMT 2026-1 involves 587 agency-eligible, conforming mortgage loans totaling around $331.1 million as of January 1, 2026. The collateral is predominantly fully amortizing, mainly 30-year fixed-rate mortgages.

data.title: Natural gas prices surge 30%, silver up 13% to $116. Cloudflare stock increases. Henry Hub exceeds $6/MMBtu. Gold also rises 2.2% to $5,100. Silver's 12-month gain at 266%. Fed suspected of currency intervention. US equities rise.
data.description: U.S. natural gas prices spiked over 30% due to Winter Storm Fern driving up heating demand and disrupting supply, leading to the largest weekly percentage gain ever for the Nymex front-month contract. The Henry Hub front-month contract now trades above $6 per million British thermal units, marking a 125% surge in four sessions from hovering around $3 just a week ago. Cloudflare stock soared, along with gold rising 2.2% to $5,100 and silver surging 13% to $116 per ounce, on track for its best one-day surge since 2008 with a rolling 12-month gain being the strongest since 1980. The dollar's weakness contributed to a broader commodity breakout. U.S. equities continued to rise, with the S&P... Read more on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stocks are an inflation hedge as companies raise prices due to higher costs, supporting earnings growth. Tariffs driving this trend as per Morgan Stanley research. Companies leveraging pricing power, bullish for earnings.
data.description: Stock market trading in publicly traded corporations is considered an inflation hedge. Companies sometimes raise prices to support earnings growth due to inflation-related costs. As per Morgan Stanley's Jan. 13 report, US firms are leveraging pricing power to pass on tariff costs to consumers, positively affecting corporate earnings and labor market risks. Q3 US GDP data and the National Federation of Independent Businesses data show evidence of firms raising prices to cover tariff costs. This strategy is seen as supportive of corporate earnings and reducing risks in the labor market. For more details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: The US dollar facing heavy pressure, DXY down 0.6% Monday, 0.9% Friday; yen surges 3% in 2 days. Suspected intervention cited.
data.description: The U.S. dollar is facing significant pressure, with its worst two-day decline since April 2025, due to speculation about currency intervention to boost the Japanese yen. The U.S. dollar index (DXY) dropped by 0.6% on Monday, following a 0.9% decline on Friday. The Japanese yen, tracked by Invesco CurrencyShares Japanese Yen Trust (FXY), surged over 3% in the last two trading sessions. Suspected intervention to sell USD/JPY led to a 3.5% drop since Friday morning, with U.S. authorities reportedly involved. Japanese authorities may have intervened after USD/JPY exceeded 159 following the Bank of Japan's policy meeting. The turbulence also stemmed from discussions in London. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Mein Street® Asian Eats wins Branding Award at 62nd GDUSA Design Awards™ in Lacey, Wash., 2026.
data.description: In Lacey, Washington, a quick-serve restaurant known as Mein Street® under the brand Real Fresh Brands™ has recently received the prestigious honor of winning the 62nd GDUSA Design Awards™. This Asian-inspired QSR brand was recognized specifically in the Branding + Identity Programs category for its outstanding brand design. Mein Street® stands out among the competition in this area and its excellence has been acknowledged through this esteemed award.

data.title: Dripos rebrands with 'Counter Talk' publication, solidifying as the OS for cafes. Announced Jan. 28, 2026. #coffee #tech
data.description: In New York on January 28, 2026, Dripos, a platform tailored for independent coffee shops, unveiled a complete rebranding effort, showcasing a fresh visual identity and introducing Counter Talk, a new industry publication. This move marks a significant step forward for the company as it aims to enhance its offerings for small coffee businesses.

data.title: PROG Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:PRG) to announce Q4 2025 financial results on Feb 18, 2026. Live webcast at 8:30 AM ET.
data.description: PROG Holdings, Inc. will announce its financial results for Q4 2025 on February 18, 2026, before the market opens. This fintech holding company for Progressive Leasing, Purchasing Power, Four Technologies, and Build is listed on NYSE as PRG. A live webcast to discuss the results will take place on February 18, 2026, at 8:30 A.M. ET. The link to access the webcast will be made available closer to the date. Stay tuned for more details.
